videos.title: Oceanview Named Title Sponsor of the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships
videos.description:
USA Pickleball is proud to announce Oceanview Life and Annuity as the new title sponsor of its premier event, now officially named the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships. The partnership marks a significant milestone for the organization and reinforces the continued momentum of pickleball as one of the fastest-growing sports in the country. 

Set to take place October 31–November

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

videos.title: Pickleball Fundamentals For Beginners
videos.description:
Having a strong foundation in pickleball fundamentals will set you up for long-term success on the court. In this video, we break down some of the most important fundamentals every beginner should learn to start playing with confidence.

First, it all starts with your grip. We recommend the continental grip, which you can find by forming a “V” with your dominant hand and placing it along the edge

videos.title: Most Important Rules to Start Playing Pickleball
videos.description:
If you don’t know the rules, you can’t play! In this video, we break down the most important pickleball rules so you can step on the court with confidence and start playing right away.

First, understand the boundaries of the court. The baselines and sidelines mark the limits, and for a ball to be in, it must land on or within these lines. If any part of the ball touches the line, it’s considered

items.title: The Overlooked Serve Tip That Adds Power Without Swinging Harder
items.summary: To add power to your pickleball serve, stop using only your arm. Start slightly closed, turn your hips and shoulders together, load the back leg, then transfer your weight forward through contact. This creates deeper, stronger serves with less effort and better control. A lot of
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should You Serve, Receive, or Choose Side?
items.summary: If you win the pickleball coin toss, don’t automatically choose serve. Pick side first if sun, wind, glare, or distractions matter. If conditions are neutral, receiving is often smart in doubles because your team can return deep, get forward, and settle into the match quickly. Mo
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Add Topspin to Your Pickleball Shots: Brush Angle and Paddle Path
items.summary: Topspin pickleball technique is built on two fundamentals: a steep brush angle and a low-to-high paddle path. Nail both, and you'll hit shots that land deep, kick high, and force your opponents into uncomfortable defensive positions.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Court Measurements: The Definitive Reference for Every Line and Zone
items.summary: Pickleball court measurements follow exact USA Pickleball standards, with a total playing surface of 44 feet long by 20 feet wide. This guide breaks down every line, zone, and dimension so you never second-guess what's in bounds or out.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Attack Dead Dinks in Pickleball: Identify and Execute Every Time
items.summary: Knowing when and how to attack dead dinks in pickleball is the fastest way to upgrade your kitchen game. This guide breaks down exactly how to identify attackable dinks and execute the right shot every time.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Senior Pickleball Doubles Strategy: Play Smarter Not Harder After 60
items.summary: Senior pickleball doubles strategy is less about speed and more about positioning, shot selection, and court IQ. Here's how players over 60 can outthink, and outplay, opponents half their age.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Use Pickleball Strategy to Beat Younger, Faster Players
items.summary: Coach Jess reveals three proven pickleball strategies that help experienced players win against younger, faster opponents through smart placement and court control rather than raw power.u
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Complete Guide to the Backhand Flick
items.summary: Stop blending your backhand shots into one confused motion. Discover how to execute a clean backhand flick pickleball shot, adjust your body mechanics, and confidently choose the right attack to win more points.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Stop Getting Attacked in Pickleball: 5 Pro Strategies
items.summary: Professional pickleball player Ashley Griffith breaks down five essential strategies to help you stop getting attacked and feel more confident during rallies. From keeping the ball low to improving your footwork, these techniques will transform how opponents pressure you on the c
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: It Took Christian Alshon 1,000+ Attempts to Hit This Dude Perfect Trickshot
items.summary: You know that "impossible" trickshot challenge where you try to put a ball through three holes that get progressively smaller? Of course you do.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: What to Eat Before Morning Pickleball Games
items.summary: Before morning pickleball, eat easy carbs with a little protein: banana with yogurt, toast with honey, oatmeal, cereal, or a small bagel. Eat a fuller meal 2–3 hours before, or a light carb snack 30–60 minutes before. Add water and electrolytes for long, hot session Morning pickl
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Biggest Block Volley Mistake in Pickleball (And Why It Kills Your Resets)
items.summary: A good block volley starts with a slightly open paddle face. If the face is too vertical or closed, the ball often drops into the net. Open it just enough to absorb pace, clear the net, and land softly in the kitchen. A lot of intermediate pickleball players think they have slow
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Forehand Crosscourt Roll That Gives Your Soft Game Teeth
items.summary: A forehand crosscourt roll is a topspin pressure shot that makes your dink game more dangerous. Hit it from balance, contact out front, stay low, brush across and up, and aim crosscourt to move opponents wide, force pop-ups, and create attacking chances without overhitting. The f
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Stay on the Pickleball Court Longer
items.summary: VKTRY Insoles are designed to return energy with every step while giving players more support and stability during long sessions on court.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Hit the Forehand 'Slap' Shot in Pickleball
items.summary: Unlike an overheard smash, the forehand slap is a compact, violent putaway motion used on balls hanging just above shoulder level.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Advanced Pickleball: How to Hit the Third Shot Roll Drop
items.summary: The third shot drop roll is one of pickleball's most advanced shots, and mastering it can transform your game. PPA pro Paula Rives walks through the exact mechanics, grip pressure, and footwork needed to execute this powerful shot consistently.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Why the Pickleball Return of Serve Is Your Most Important Shot
items.summary: The pickleball return of serve is often overlooked, but it's arguably the most critical shot in the game. PPA pro Kelly Goodnow explains why a deep, controlled return sets up your entire point.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Master Pickleball Anticipation Like a Pro Player
items.summary: Professional pickleball players seem to know where every shot is going before it happens. By mastering pickleball anticipation through reading body language, paddle positioning, and shot patterns, you can start seeing the game like the pros do.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 9 New Pickleball Paddles Worth a Second Look
items.summary: With the paddle market moving so fast these days, you'd be forgiven for not knowing what's new and noteworthy. Fear not – we've got you covered.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: First Look: Selkirk Omni Debuts as Successor to the Boomstik
items.summary: An all-court, full-foam paddle that will debut in a range of shapes and colors.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: Should Better Players Be Expected to Teach During Open Play?
items.summary: Better players are not obligated to teach during regular pickleball open play. They should be respectful, safe, and helpful when asked, but beginners should not expect free coaching. The best rule: ask before giving advice, offer one quick tip if invited, and keep the game moving
items.author: Ana

items.title: Why Better Players Hold the Ball Longer Before Hitting
items.summary: Holding shots longer in pickleball means preparing early, then delaying contact slightly so your dink, speedup, or redirect looks similar longer. For intermediate players, this creates disguise, makes opponents move early, improves decision-making, and helps you attack or dink ba
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Use the Down-the-Line Shot as a Doubles Weapon
items.summary: A good down-the-line shot is earned, not forced. Use it when a poacher cheats middle, you’re pulled wide, or you can attack a backhand. Aim inside the sideline, hit with controlled pace, and shift with your partner so the next ball doesn’t burn you. Down the line is one of those
items.author: Ana

items.title: Major League Pickleball Dallas: Pools, schedule and viewing guide
items.summary: Major League Pickleball will hold its season-opening event this weekend outside of Dallas. There are 11 teams in action, and the event will mark the debut of a new format this year. More

items.title: How to Cover the Middle Without Stepping on Your Partner’s Toes
items.summary: To cover the middle in pickleball doubles, use the forehand as the default, but let balance, court position, and shot quality decide. Talk before points, move as a unit, shade middle when your partner is pulled wide, and avoid reaching across their space unless you can take the b
items.author: Ana

items.title: Can You Pass the Pickleball Consistency Test?
items.summary: A simple pickleball consistency benchmark: miss no more than 1 serve and 1 return per game, keep 70% of third shots neutral or unattackable, and limit routine unforced errors to 1–2 per game. Drills help, but real consistency shows up under pressure. “Be more consistent.” It migh
items.author: Ana
